The NIS2 Cybersecurity Act is coming and goes further than you think. The new European NIS2 directive is a fundamental shift in how organizations should approach risks. metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: ABN AMRO Editorial. The NIS2 Cybersecurity Act is coming and goes further than you think. The new European NIS2 directive is a fundamental shift in how organizations should approach risks. Not only digital risks such as hacking or data leaks, but also risks that could endanger the continuity of an essential or important entity. This is called the 'all hazards approach'.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: The NIS2 Cybersecurity Act is coming and goes further than you think. The new European NIS2 directive is a fundamental shift in how organizations should approach risks. Not only digital risks such as hacking or data leaks, but also risks that could endanger the continuity of an essential or important entity. This is called the 'all hazards approach'. This means: IT, OT, IoT and physical risks in digital environments.

text:
In understandable language. With clear examples. So that as an organization, as a supplier or as a CISO, you know what to do. IT risks - Information Technology. The classics, think of:

So that as an organization, as a supplier or as a CISO, you know what to do. IT risks - Information Technology. The classics, think of:

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text:
IT risks - Information Technology. The classics, think of:
[Ransomware](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/wat-is-ransomware.html): systems are encrypted, the operation is paralyzed. Data leaks: customer data or medical records are stolen or leaked. Software that is not maintained: updates are not performed and older software is very vulnerable. Insecure API connections: for example in companies that use EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) to digitally exchange orders, invoices or stock information.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text:
The classics, think of:
[Ransomware](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/cybersecurity/wat-is-ransomware.html): systems are encrypted, the operation is paralyzed. Data leaks: customer data or medical records are stolen or leaked. Software that is not maintained: updates are not performed and older software is very vulnerable. Insecure API connections: for example in companies that use EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) to digitally exchange orders, invoices or stock information. These are often not IT companies, but ordinary logistics service providers, raw material suppliers or suppliers.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: A whole new area of ​​insecurity opens up here. Risks don't stop at the walls of your company. Software systems and access often extend far beyond the walls of your own company. The NIS2 guideline requires a broad view. You must identify risks, not only based on company type, but based on what a company does within your chain.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: Risks don't stop at the walls of your company. Software systems and access often extend far beyond the walls of your own company. The NIS2 guideline requires a broad view. You must identify risks, not only based on company type, but based on what a company does within your chain. As the National Cyber ​​Security Center (NCSC) also states in measure 7 about chain dependency: 'Identify risks in the chain and take measures that suit your chain'.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: Software systems and access often extend far beyond the walls of your own company. The NIS2 guideline requires a broad view. You must identify risks, not only based on company type, but based on what a company does within your chain. As the National Cyber ​​Security Center (NCSC) also states in measure 7 about chain dependency: 'Identify risks in the chain and take measures that suit your chain'. It takes some getting used to for CISOs, lawyers and companies that already have ISO27001.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: The NIS2 guideline requires a broad view. You must identify risks, not only based on company type, but based on what a company does within your chain. It takes some getting used to for CISOs, lawyers and companies that already have ISO27001. The NIS2 guideline is a wake-up call.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

It takes some getting used to for CISOs, lawyers and companies that already have ISO27001. The NIS2 guideline is a wake-up call. Unfortunately, you cannot cover everything with policy and documentation.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: As the National Cyber ​​Security Center (NCSC) also states in measure 7 about chain dependency: 'Identify risks in the chain and take measures that suit your chain'. It takes some getting used to for CISOs, lawyers and companies that already have ISO27001. The NIS2 guideline is a wake-up call. Unfortunately, you cannot cover everything with policy and documentation. The reality is more complex: suppliers have access, systems are interconnected, physical and digital worlds are intertwined.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: NIS2 = all hazards. NIS2's '[all hazards](https://samendigitaalveilig.nl/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/All-hazards-examples.jpg)' approach makes sense. And necessary. Not every risk is the standard hacker you expect. Sometimes it is a forgotten USB stick, a subcontractor with outdated software or a refrigerated truck with a poorly secured IoT sensor.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text:
NIS2's '[all hazards](https://samendigitaalveilig.nl/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/All-hazards-examples.jpg)' approach makes sense. And necessary. Not every risk is the standard hacker you expect. Sometimes it is a forgotten USB stick, a subcontractor with outdated software or a refrigerated truck with a poorly secured IoT sensor. If you want to be demonstrably safe, you must:
Understand where you are vulnerable – digitally, operationally and physically.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text:
And necessary. Not every risk is the standard hacker you expect. Sometimes it is a forgotten USB stick, a subcontractor with outdated software or a refrigerated truck with a poorly secured IoT sensor. If you want to be demonstrably safe, you must:
Understand where you are vulnerable – digitally, operationally and physically. Knowing who your partners and suppliers are – and how safely they work.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text:
Not every risk is the standard hacker you expect. Sometimes it is a forgotten USB stick, a subcontractor with outdated software or a refrigerated truck with a poorly secured IoT sensor. If you want to be demonstrably safe, you must:
Understand where you are vulnerable – digitally, operationally and physically. Knowing who your partners and suppliers are – and how safely they work. Manage the risks, appropriate for your chain, industry and role.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text:
Sometimes it is a forgotten USB stick, a subcontractor with outdated software or a refrigerated truck with a poorly secured IoT sensor. If you want to be demonstrably safe, you must:
Understand where you are vulnerable – digitally, operationally and physically. Knowing who your partners and suppliers are – and how safely they work. Manage the risks, appropriate for your chain, industry and role. NIS2 is all-hazards and that is not a threat, but an opportunity to really make your entire chain safer.

metadata.source_title: Cyber ​​risks in the chain - what does that actually look like?

text: Give them a heads up in advance to implement the NIS2 Cybersecurity Act. Take cyber measures yourself or report fraud? About 1 in 5 entrepreneurs is confronted with cybercrime. Such a cyber incident can have major consequences for business operations, customers, suppliers or reputation. Taking appropriate measures is therefore important for every company.

text:
About 1 in 5 entrepreneurs is confronted with cybercrime. Such a cyber incident can have major consequences for business operations, customers, suppliers or reputation. Taking appropriate measures is therefore important for every company. Learn more about the 4-in-1 cyber solution [Cyber ​​Safe & Secure](https://doorpassen.abnamro.nl/producten-en-diensten/cyber-veilig-en-zeker/?icmp=cm-od_article-insights_btn-link) and reduce your cyber risks. View smart tools, useful articles and cyber webinars on our [Safe entrepreneurship page](https://doorpassen.abnamro.nl/producten-en-diensten/veilig-ondernemen/?icmp=cm-od_article-insights_btn-link).
[Report fraud to us](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/prive/abnamro/veilig-banken/fraude-melden.html "Report fraud"), even if in doubt, after which our helpdesk will help you immediately.
